title: Protocol for removing ssDNA from dsDNA or RNA Samples (NEB #M0568)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7odut","text":"The protocol described below will enable degradation of up to 20 pmol of a 25 nt ssDNA (~ 200 ng). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1u9rh","text":"In order to degrade larger amounts of ssDNA or ssDNAs longer than 25 nt, we recommend adding more enzyme instead of extending the reaction time. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bc7p8","text":"Users should note that ssDNAs longer than 25 nt may form secondary structures that hinder Thermolabile Exonuclease I activity.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"6v14f","text":"Please see SDS (Safety Data Sheet) for hazards and safety warnings.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare a 20 µL reaction as follows: %s %s *Most PCR buffers are compatible. **No reaction buffer is necessary if enzyme is added to a PCR reaction. ***Scale larger reaction volumes proportionally.
Stop reaction by heat-inactivation at 80 °C for 00:01:00 .
creator: New England   Biolabs
